data.description:
# BUG: same-field clobber has no conflict record

## Reproduction Steps
1. Bind a ticket; let one pass sync it (baseline established).
2. Edit the ticket's title locally AND edit the same issue's summary in Jira, between two passes.
3. Run reconcile.

**Expected:** the both-sides conflict is recorded (a conflict comment / BRIDGE_ALERT) so the overwrite is visible.
**Actual:** local wins and Jira's edit is silently overwritten with no conflict record; an absent
baseline widens the window ("degrade to local-wins").

## What / files
- `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/outbound_fields.py` (`_local_matches_prev` / `_diff_fields`) — detect `local != baseline AND jira != baseline` on the 5 mirrored scalars.
- `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/apply_outbound.py:207-232` (outbound/conflict leaf) — emit the conflict signal.
Sharpens once B1 makes the per-binding baseline the live arbiter.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A true both-sides field conflict emits a conflict record (comment/alert) instead of silently overwriting.
- [ ] Local-wins is preserved as the resolution; only observability is added (no behavior regression).
- [ ] Regression test covering the both-sides-diverged case.
data.title: Silent same-field clobber: outbound local-wins overwrites a Jira-side edit with no conflict signal
